frontpage.headline: Top global leaders to attend India-AI Impact Summit in New Delhi next week
frontpage.summary: An AI Impact Summit bringing together heads of state and global tech leaders is scheduled in New Delhi in early July. India is positioning the event as a declaration of its role in shaping the global AI agenda, not just adopting it.
frontpage.takeaway: India is moving from AI importer to AI agenda-setter — New Delhi next week is where that story gets written.
frontpage.why: Policy announcements from this summit will likely set the direction of India's AI regulatory posture, public procurement priorities, and global partnership terms for the next two to three years. If you are tracking where to position in the Indian AI ecosystem, the summit's outcomes are worth following closely.

frontpage.headline: Climate science confirms human emissions supercharged Europe's second record heatwave in two months
frontpage.summary: Western and central Europe has broken heat records for the second time in eight weeks. Attribution scientists confirm that human-caused warming made this heatwave substantially more intense and more probable. The UK broke its June temperature record for three consecutive days; France postponed public events; Poland issued wildfire warnings.
frontpage.takeaway: Back-to-back attribution-confirmed records mean Europe's unprecedented heat is now the baseline, not the exception.
frontpage.why: India has direct exposure: European logistics slowdowns affect Indian exporters; climate migration from affected regions will reshape geopolitics; and rapid attribution science is tightening legal liability on emitters worldwide. Useful context for anyone thinking about climate risk in business strategy.

frontpage.headline: India startup funding tops $1.09 billion this week, up 290% year-on-year
frontpage.summary: A CRED mega-round anchored an exceptional week for Indian startup funding, pushing the weekly total past $1 billion for the first time in years. Year-on-year the jump is 290%, though CRED's outsized round means the headline number flatters the broader ecosystem signal.
frontpage.takeaway: Adjust for the CRED effect, but a 290% year-on-year surge still signals serious capital returning to Indian tech.
frontpage.why: Consumer fintech at scale is back in favour with investors — CRED's raise is the clearest proof. The next two weeks of deal flow will show whether this is a one-off or the start of a fresh cycle. For someone building or advising in the ecosystem, this is a good moment to watch which sectors follow.

stories.headline: India's pharma industry is tariff-proof but faces a deeper China dependency problem
stories.summary: An analysis in The Diplomat argues that India's generic pharma sector has successfully positioned itself beyond US tariff risk, but remains acutely dependent on Chinese active pharmaceutical ingredients. The piece traces how this dependency emerged and why substituting it is harder than Indian manufacturers admit.
stories.takeaway: India's pharma resilience story has a hidden vulnerability: the supply chain runs through China, not around it.
stories.why: For anyone tracking India's industrial strategy or healthcare supply chains, this is the honest version of the India as global pharmacy narrative. The API dependency is a strategic risk that both government and investors are underweighting. It is also an opportunity: whoever solves domestic API manufacturing in India at scale builds a genuinely strategic asset.

stories.headline: Cold chain infrastructure is the missing link holding back India's next food economy
stories.summary: An ET Hospitality analysis argues that India's food economy is constrained less by production and more by the absence of integrated cold chain infrastructure. Post-harvest losses remain among the world's highest, limiting the value farmers can capture and the products India can export at quality.
stories.takeaway: India wastes roughly 16-18% of food post-harvest — cold chain is not a logistics problem, it is an economic opportunity.
stories.why: For anyone in agritech, food, or infrastructure, cold chain is one of the highest-leverage gaps in the Indian food system. The constraint is not capital scarcity but fragmented, unconnected facilities. A platform that integrates cold storage, transport and last-mile delivery at district level could unlock enormous value. Practically actionable for a founder.

stories.headline: India-UK free trade agreement aims for transformational growth, 1,000 advisors deployed
stories.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal described the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement as a vehicle for transformational growth for both economies. The government is deploying 1,000 advisory personnel and upgrading its trade portal to help Indian businesses leverage the deal.
stories.takeaway: India-UK CETA is signed — now comes the harder work of actually using it, which is where most FTAs fail.
stories.why: For Indian businesses and startups, the advisory infrastructure is the actionable angle: 1,000 trade advisors and a new portal mean tangible support for identifying UK market opportunities. If you are building anything with potential UK enterprise customers, this is the moment to explore what CETA actually offers your sector.

stories.headline: OECD confirms India remains the world's fastest-growing major economy
stories.summary: A new OECD report reaffirms India's position as the fastest-growing major economy, maintaining its growth trajectory even as global headwinds persist from trade tensions and AI-related inflation risks.
stories.takeaway: India's macro story is still the best available among large economies — and the OECD is now saying so explicitly.
stories.why: This puts India in a stronger negotiating position in trade and investment discussions — India-UK CETA, the Japan summit, the AI Impact Summit — when growth fundamentals are confirmed by multilateral bodies. For someone positioned toward India-focused work, useful validation.

opportunities.name: First-Ever National Rural Development Conference
opportunities.summary: Inaugural national conference on rural development held in Delhi, bringing together policymakers, practitioners and researchers on India's rural economy. Relevant for anyone working on agritech, rural fintech, or India's development agenda. Delhi, national.

opportunities.name: SGSITS Incubation Forum Cohort 3.0 — Startup Grant Program
opportunities.summary: Third cohort of SGSITS Incubation Forum's startup grant program for early-stage ventures in India. Worth a look if you are early-stage or advising founders who are. National.
